talks.title: OSEC Forum 2024 - Migration from RH SSO to RHBK
talks.summary: Presentation outlining major shift in internal architecture of Keycloak project and underlying Red Hat product offered on top of it.
talks.description: Presentation outlining major shift in internal architecture of Keycloak project and underlying Red Hat product offered on top of it.

talks.title: OSEC Forum 2023 - Keycloak in less common scenarios
talks.summary: A quick intro to OAuth/OpenID Connect and supported authorization flows such as Device Flow.
talks.description: A quick intro to OAuth/OpenID Connect and supported authorization flows such as Device Flow.

talks.title: ApacheCon Home@2021: Unified fieldbus API with Apache PLC4X
talks.summary: An introduction to fieldbus technologies and how Apache PLC4X provides a unified API for industrial protocols such as Profinet, CANopen, and Ethernet/IP.
talks.description: A talk about fieldbus technologies and how Apache PLC4X provides a unified API to access industrial communication protocols.

cover.alt: Video thumbnail for ApacheCon Home 2021 talk about Apache PLC4X

talks.title: ApacheCon Asia@2021: Apache PLC4X and CANopen
talks.summary: Introduction to CAN API and CANopen standard and its implementation within Apache PLC4X project. Presentation prepared during pandemic for ApacheCon Asia 2021.
talks.description: Introduction to CAN API and the CANopen standard with an implementation perspective through Apache PLC4X.

cover.alt: Video thumbnail for ApacheCon Asia 2021 talk about Apache PLC4X and CANopen

talks.title: EclipseCon@2018: Presentation - Developer productivity with Maven+OSGi
talks.summary: During this short presentation I will revisit existing mechanisms and approach towards OSGi and JEE development. I will show how many manual steps can be avoided and how to maintain project in effective manner. I will try to find a balance between execution environment requirements and programmer happines at same time.
talks.description: A presentation about reducing manual work in OSGi and JEE development and improving developer productivity with Maven.

cover.alt: Video thumbnail for EclipseCon 2018 talk about Maven and OSGi

talks.title: EclipseCon@2018: Panel Discussion - Build Tooling for Eclipse SmartHome
talks.summary: A discussion within Eclipse SmartHome project involving project leader as well as recognized personalities within OSGi universe on how to approach problem of making developer life easier.
talks.description: A panel discussion on build tooling choices and developer experience in the Eclipse SmartHome ecosystem.

cover.alt: Video thumbnail for EclipseCon 2018 panel discussion about build tooling

talks.title: EclipseCon@2018: SmartHome Day - OSGi+Plain Old Maven
talks.summary: A brief thought experiment exploring why OSGi is often not a preferred choice and how to make it more approachable for everyday Java developers. The presentation is based on proof of concept build for Eclipse SmartHome—a large OSGi-based project—using Apache Maven. With no use of Eclipse Tycho. Demonstration of a simpler and more developer-friendly approach.
talks.description: A talk exploring a simpler Maven-based approach to working with OSGi in Eclipse SmartHome without relying on Tycho.

cover.alt: Video thumbnail for EclipseCon 2018 SmartHome Day talk about OSGi and Maven

talks.title: ApacheCon NA 2014 - Apache Karaf in real life
talks.summary: A talk about using Apache Karaf in real-world solutions, covering modularity, extensibility, OSGi, and lessons learned from production use.
talks.description: Presentation about Apache Karaf in practical use, including OSGi fundamentals, extensibility patterns, Karaf-based solution design, and production lessons learned.

cover.alt: Video thumbnail for ApacheCon NA 2014 talk Apache Karaf in real life

talks.title: Developer Conference 2013 - Application integration with SwitchYard
talks.summary: Talk about application integration with SwitchYard.
talks.description: Talk listed on the Developer Conference 2013 speaker directory for Łukasz Dywicki.

talks.title: ApacheCon Europe 2012 - When Camel meets CDI
talks.summary: A talk about using CDI with Apache Camel in Java EE and Java SE environments, covering dependency injection basics, extensions, and Camel integration without XML.
talks.description: Presentation on how CDI and Apache Camel work together, including CDI basics, custom extensions, Camel integration with dependency injection containers, and running a simple Camel application on an application server without XML.
